description: Widespread Panic's official "Porch Songs" archival live-release series (widespreadpanic.com): specific historic shows, mostly the Michael Houser era, each with the band's own highlight notes. Facts + short highlight snippet + a listen link; full text stays on the band's site. Use to decorate Tour-in-Review, especially 1991-2002.

entries.title: Porch Songs: San Francisco 1992
entries.highlights: It’s the spring of 1992, San Francisco. In the years to come the Warfield Theater will become hallowed ground for live Panic, but on this night the band fills the support slot for their fellow H.O.R.D.E. compatriots, Phish. It’s a power-packed set and the band leaves it all on the stage. Travel back and witness another snapshot in the live history of Widespread Panic. 4/17/1992 Warfield Theater San Francisco, CA

entries.title: Porch Songs: Pittsburgh 1995
entries.highlights: Smack-dab in the middle of a massive cross-country tour (promoting the only seven months old Ain’t Life Grand ), Panic’s Tuesday night stop in Pittsburgh found a super rowdy crowd raring to go. The band is loose but locked in, delivering two strong and high-energy sets. More info over in the Archive . Download Pittsburgh 1995

entries.title: Porch Songs: Oxford 1995
entries.highlights: “The band routinely made a stop in Oxford from 1989-1992 and then played there for the last time this night in 1995, a year that seemed to help musically bridge the band from its earlier years to 1996 and beyond…but of course it did.” ( Archive Blog ) The fifteenth installment of the live archive two-track releases: 9/22/95 Tad Smith Coliseum, Oxford, Mississippi Available on CD/MP3/FLAC/ALAC at LiveWidespreadPanic.com

entries.title: Porch Songs: Omaha, NE 1997
entries.highlights: Omaha 1997 finds the show held at the Ranch Bowl, on a sand volleyball court in the middle of the summer. Highlights from this show include the first ever performance of “Bear’s Gone Fishing” (instrumental), seamless segues in both the first and second sets, and a “Feelin’ Alright” encore.

entries.title: Porch Songs: Louisville 1997
entries.highlights: “If you’ve never been to the Louisville Palace, you should consider finding a reason…and that won’t be tough to do especially if Widespread Panic returns to this 2,700 seat masterpiece of a venue. Upon entering, all it takes is a long look at the ceiling and you just know you’re in the right place…no doubt about it. And then the music starts…” May 7th, 1997 is available now in MP3, WAV and CD formats to add to your WP Archive collection. Check out the full show synopsis over at the Archive .

entries.title: Porch Songs: Kalamazoo 1997
entries.highlights: The thirteenth in the series of live two-track releases comes out today on LiveWidespreadPanic.com . November 11th, 1997 found the band right in the middle of a long Fall Tour at the State Theater in Kalamazoo. An early rendition of “One Kind Favor” as well as the third ever performance of “You’ll Be Fine” are mixed into a well-rounded setlist packed into six inches of snow out in the early Michigan winter. Read up on the show breakdown over at the Archive blog , and

entries.title: Porch Songs: Atlanta, Ga 1998
entries.highlights: “The four-night New Year’s run in 1997 at the Fox Theatre not only ushered in 1998, it also gave us our first show of 1998…and also the last show in the states before Widespread Panic took off to Australia and Europe…to come back for some Panic in the Streets in April. The decision to spread the four Fox shows over five days was genius…build up to New Years, take a night off on New Year’s day to recover from the celebration, and then gather again on the 2nd to see what happens. Well, what happened was just another incredib…

entries.title: Porch Songs: Bozeman 2000
entries.highlights: Announcing the thirty-fourth installment of the band’s two-track ‘Porch Songs’ live series: Bozeman 2000. It’s about time we came back to 2000 and seeing as we’ve had our eye on this show for a while, what better way to kick off this years series! While we had hoped this could have been a multi-track release, there were unfortunately some recording errors on those tapes that could not be corrected. Lucky for us, the two-track front-of-house recording is crisp, clear and with plenty of audience captured on to the recording. So w…

entries.title: Porch Songs: Memphis 2000
entries.venue: and 11/25/2000 . Horace highlights the shows moment-to-moment over in the Archive . And w
entries.highlights: The next installment of the “Porch Songs” live archive series finds the band near the tail-end of its fall tour in the year 2000 with a two-night run in Memphis, Tennessee. Mid-South Coliseum would see the band return each fall for the three fall tours to follow, but was their first ever appearance at the venue. Get your copy of 11/24/2000 and 11/25/2000 . Horace highlights the shows moment-to-moment over in the Archive . And while you’re there, check out the ever-growing list of live releases spanning as far back as ’89 an…

entries.title: Porch Songs: Bozeman 2001
entries.highlights: The 19th installment of the Widespread Panic Archive “Porch Song” show series is now available on CD and download. Bozeman 2001 was a week into the band’s fall tour, falling on a Friday in big sky country at the Brick Breeden Fieldhouse on the campus of Montana State University with two sets that just won’t quit, plus a fired-up sit-in by Jerry Joseph with Brad Rosen on percussion and Junior Ruppel joining in on vocals. “It always takes extra effort and dedication to get to those special destinations…and the band always see…

entries.title: Porch Songs: Red Rocks 2001
entries.highlights: Among the many highlights of this run, a few stand-outs include: the very first “Don’t Tell the Band”, sit-ins by the Dickinson brothers and Cecil Daniels, first “Action Man” with lyrics, and the only “Boogie Chillen”. Every Red Rocks performance is special for both fan and band. Read Horace Moore’s synopsis of the shows over at the Archive Blog . 6/22/2001 – Red Rocks Amphitheatre – Morrison, CO 6/23/2001 – Red Rocks Amphitheatre – Morrison, CO 6/24/2001 – Red Rocks Amphitheatre – Morrison, CO

entries.title: Porch Songs: Geneva, MN – 7/4/2001
entries.venue: “We’ve returned once again to 2001…this time for the 8th volume of Porch Songs…and for
entries.highlights: “We’ve returned once again to 2001…this time for the 8th volume of Porch Songs…and for quite the 4th of July celebration.”

entries.title: Porch Songs: Wilmington, DE 2001 – CD Orders and Art Now Available!
entries.highlights: The third installment of the Porch Songs, the two-track live releases from the Widespread Panic Archive, is now available at www.LiveWidespreadPanic.com . Additionally, CD formats are now available for all Porch Songs releases , as well as downloadable artwork! 7/22/01 – Kahuna Summer Stage – Wilmington, DE Read Horace’s run-down of the show here .
